repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Closes #39213

### Problem

`ex_detach()` has a stub for `:detach!` that errors with `"bang (!) not supported yet"`. There is no way for one UI to kick the others off a shared Nvim server.

### Solution

`:detach!` disconnects every UI except the caller's, keeping the server running and the caller attached. No-op with one UI. Errors with `E5768: No UI attached` when no UI is attached.

comment_data.body: can this be implemented in Lua? `_core/server.lua`

I couldn't find an existing way in Lua to cleanly detach another UI. `vim.fn.chanclose(chan)` gets close, but doesn't set `chan->detach`, so if one of the others is a stdio UI (e.g. an --embed parent), closing it exits the server.

`nvim_ui_detach` and `nvim__chan_set_detach` are FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY, so Lua can only invoke them against the caller's own channel.

To move this to `_core/server.lua`, I'd need to add a new primitive (something like `nvim__ui_detach(chan)`) that sets `chan->detach = true`, sends `error_exit`, and closes the channel.

Does this sound correct, or do you have a different approach in mind?
I've had a go at this in 6bc5831, happy to revise if there is a better approach.
this shouldn't be needed...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -108,6 +109,28 @@ void remote_ui_disconnect(uint64_t channel_id, Error *err, bool send_error_exit)
   remote_ui_destroy(ui);
 }
 
+/// Detaches the UI on channel `chan`.
+///
+/// Sets the channel's detach flag (so the server doesn't exit on stdio UIs),
+/// sends an "error_exit" UI event, and closes the channel.
+///
+/// @param chan  UI channel to disconnect.
+/// @param[out] err Error details, if any.
+void nvim__ui_detach(Integer chan, Error *err)

comment_data.body: there is already `nvim__chan_set_detach()`. maybe it should be updated to take a `chan` argument as the first param.
no need to track this number. instead, return `uicount - #vim.api.nvim_list_uis()`, that's the actual source of truth
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -38,4 +38,21 @@ function M.serverlist(opts, addrs)
   return addrs
 end
 
+--- Disconnects every UI except `keep_chan`, keeping the server running.
+---
+--- @param keep_chan integer Channel ID of the UI to preserve.
+--- @return integer # Number of UIs detached.
+function M.detach_others(keep_chan)
+  local detached = 0
+  for _, ui in ipairs(vim.api.nvim_list_uis()) do
+    if ui.chan and ui.chan ~= keep_chan then
+      -- Set detach first, so closing a stdio UI's channel doesn't exit the server.
+      vim.api.nvim__chan_set_detach(ui.chan, true)
+      vim.fn.chanclose(ui.chan)
+      detached = detached + 1

@@ -38,4 +38,21 @@ function M.serverlist(opts, addrs)
   return addrs
 end
 
+--- Disconnects every UI except `keep_chan`, keeping the server running.
+---
+--- @param keep_chan integer Channel ID of the UI to preserve.
+--- @return integer # Number of UIs detached.
+function M.detach_others(keep_chan)
+  local detached = 0
+  for _, ui in ipairs(vim.api.nvim_list_uis()) do
+    if ui.chan and ui.chan ~= keep_chan then
+      -- Set detach first, so closing a stdio UI's channel doesn't exit the server.
+      vim.api.nvim__chan_set_detach(ui.chan, true)
+      vim.fn.chanclose(ui.chan)
+      detached = detached + 1
+    end
+  end
+  return detached

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  local uicount = #vim.api.nvim_list_uis()
```

comment_data.body: no need to change this
no
just always use plural "UIs", keep it simple
this can probably be an assert at the right place in the above test, and that saves a lot of code.

@@ -201,6 +203,83 @@ describe('TUI :detach', function()
       {5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
     ]])
   end)

+    setup_detach_child()
+    finally(function()
+      pcall(function()

+

+
+    local verify_session = n.connect(child_server)
+    local _, uis_after = verify_session:request('nvim_list_uis')
+    eq(1, #uis_after)
+    eq(tui_chan_id, uis_after[1].chan)
+  end)
+

comment_data.body: why not do the messages in Lua? it can still return `n`, for the `ILOG` below. though soon we'll have `vim.log`: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38906
addressed in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39216/commits/3cf5f599b55656665f59664445c814bf1c1849db

since the screen contents mostly aren't relevant to these tests, can make this more concise:

    screen:expect({ any = vim.pesc('[Hello from detach!%^') })

-end)
 
-describe('TUI :restart', function()
-  before_each(n.clear)
-  after_each(n.check_close)

-    -- Retry: old server may still be alive (connect succeeds but yields stale starttime),

comment_data.body: I think this comment should be removed.
i like it and want it to stay
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dUn5-8EC_DQ

comment_regular_data.body:
thanks. +1 for the idea, see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39213#issuecomment-4276096004 

and please update this PR after reviewing https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work

Thanks, I've updated the info messages, and attempted to move `remote_ui_disconnect_others` into `server.lua`.

I've also reviewed the AI assisted work section, please let me know if anything else stands out.

@justinmk
Addressed your 2 points in [73ba066](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39216/commits/73ba06634a94f9f5ea5a00ed999b5f189bad0672)

- Added `chan` param to `nvim__chan_set_detach` (`chan=0` falls back to caller for `:restart`'s RPC use)
- Dropped `FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY`
- Deleted `nvim__ui_detach`
- Moved the flow into `detach_others()`

Note: Claude also flagged that `rpc_close_event` calls `remote_ui_disconnect(..., false)`, so detached clients no longer get an explicit `error_exit` event before close.
can we get this over the line, what's remaining? spent a lot of time reviewing this, let's please finish it

@justinmk everything is good on my end. I believe I've addressed all of your points unless there is anything else you'd like me to do?
I've also been dogfooding it over the last few weeks and it's working nicely.
oh, it's still in Draft though
my bad, ready for your review!
@justinmk let me know if you need anything else for this

labels.description: May contain trace amounts of GMO
labels.name: AI assisted 🤖

title: feat(ui): implement :detach! to disconnect other UIs
